intro: Tapeinia flava, also known as Yellow Tapeinia, is a perennial plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Cucurbitaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Tapeinia flava include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
